title: TensorRT: The AI Compiler
concept: NVIDIA TensorRT is an SDK for high-performance deep learning inference that compiles models for optimal speed on NVIDIA GPUs.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What role does ONNX play in machine learning pipelines?

options.text: It acts as a universal bridge format between different frameworks
It speeds up training using automatic data augmentation
It labels images automatically using zero-shot learning

exercises.explain: ONNX defines a standardized representation of the model graph, facilitating cross-platform deployment.

cards.title: Compiling your neural network
cards.body: When you write python code, it runs step-by-step. But when you want to fly, you compile it. NVIDIA's TensorRT acts like a compiler specifically for neural networks, reshaping them to squeeze every ounce of performance out of NVIDIA GPUs.

cards.title: What is TensorRT?
cards.body: TensorRT is a hardware-specific optimization engine. It takes a model (like an ONNX graph), analyzes the target GPU architecture, and compiles it into an optimized engine by fusing layers and selecting the fastest kernels.

cards.title: Custom puzzle pieces
cards.body: Think of a generic model as a set of standard building blocks. TensorRT takes these blocks and melts them down into custom-molded pieces that fit perfectly into the specific layout of your GPU's computing units.

cards.title: Compiling ONNX to TensorRT
cards.body: An automated drone team converts an ONNX detector model to TensorRT. On the NVIDIA Jetson edge GPU, the TensorRT compiled version runs 4x faster and consumes significantly less battery power.

cards.title: Hardware specificity is key
cards.body: You might think you can compile a TensorRT engine on your laptop GPU and run it on a server GPU. In reality, TensorRT engines are highly hardware-specific: you must compile them on the exact GPU model they will run on.

cards.title: TensorRT Compiler Optimization
cards.body: How TensorRT restructures models for execution:

left.text:
Separate convolution layers
Separate activation layers
Multiple memory transfers

right.text:
Fused Conv+ReLU layers
Hardware-selected kernels
Reduced memory overhead

exercises.prompt: Which hardware brand is TensorRT specifically designed to optimize for?
exercises.explain: TensorRT is built by NVIDIA specifically to optimize deep learning inference on NVIDIA GPU architectures.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A compiled TensorRT engine can be run on any CPU without a GPU.
exercises.explain: False. TensorRT engines are compiled specifically for NVIDIA GPUs and require them to execute.

exercises.sentence: TensorRT optimizes inference by ___ layers together to reduce memory overhead.
exercises.explain: Layer fusion combines multiple mathematical operations into a single step, saving time and memory transfers.

exercises.prompt: What happens during the compilation step of TensorRT?

options.text: It profiles target hardware to choose the fastest GPU kernels
It adds extra weight parameters to the model layers
It automatically generates synthetic training datasets

exercises.explain: TensorRT measures kernel performance on the local GPU to build the most efficient execution plan.

exercises.prompt: You want to deploy an object detector on an NVIDIA server and need to minimize latency. You have exported your model to ONNX. What is the recommended next step?

options.text: Compile the ONNX model into a TensorRT engine on the target server GPU.
Convert the model back to PyTorch and run it inside a standard Python script.
Compile a TensorRT engine on a different GPU, then copy it over to the server.
Avoid compilation because Python execution is always faster than native GPU kernels.

exercises.explain: Compiling the ONNX model into a hardware-specific TensorRT engine on the target server GPU maximizes performance and minimizes latency.
